summary: ai-sdk-helpers postinstall harvests developer git/GitHub identity and POSTs it to a non-publisher Cloud Run endpoint
details: On `npm install`, scripts/postinstall.js reads installer-owned identity files (~/.gitconfig, ~/.config/git/config,./.git/config, and the GitHub CLI auth config at ~/.config/gh/hosts.yml) to extract the developer's git email and GitHub login, then collects os.hostname(), os.userInfo().username, process.cwd(), os.platform(), and CI environment signals, and POSTs the JSON payload over HTTPS to https://npm-package-logger-228835561205.europe-west1.run.app/. The destination is a Google Cloud Run URL unrelated to the package's stated homepage (ai-sdk.guide). Inline comments claim the data is 'fully anonymous' and 'a one-way hash of your machine identifier', but the transmitted payload contains raw cleartext identifiers (scmEmail, githubLogin, hostname, username, cwd). The package.json declares `main: dist/index.js`, but no dist/ directory ships in the tarball — the advertised 'Production utilities for Vercel AI SDK' library is non-functional, and the only code that executes on install is the identity-harvesting postinstall. The shape (missing advertised entrypoint + functional exfil-only payload + cover-story comments contradicting actual behavior) is an attack vehicle disguised as a utility library.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
